etymology.description: 奇(奇) (qí, "strange; odd") denotes something unusual or out of the ordinary. 怪(怪) (guài, "odd; strange; to blame") further emphasizes strangeness or peculiarity, and can also be associated with moral judgment or assigning blame.
etymology.interpretation: The word 奇怪 (qíguài) combines two characters that both relate to concepts of irregularity and difference from the norm. 奇(奇) (qí, "strange; odd") refers to something that stands out due to its rarity or surprising nature. 怪(怪) (guài, "odd; strange; to blame") adds an additional layer, not only marking something as strange but also featuring a sense of moral judgment or emotional reaction, like something being so unusual it may invoke curiosity or suspicion. Together, they form a phrase that strongly conveys a sense of strangeness or peculiarity.

examples.chinese: 这个地方有很多奇怪的声音(這個地方有很多奇怪的聲音)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège dìfāng yǒu hěn duō qíguài de shēngyīn.
examples.english: There are many strange sounds in this place.

examples.chinese: 他今天的行为很奇怪(他今天的行為很奇怪)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jīntiān de xíngwéi hěn qíguài.
examples.english: His behavior today is very strange.

examples.chinese: 我觉得这件事情有点奇怪(我覺得這件事情有點奇怪)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhè jiàn shìqíng yǒu diǎn qíguài.
examples.english: I think this matter is a bit strange.
